templates.name: Node + TypeScript
templates.summary: Node 22 with pnpm, TypeScript, and common JS tooling for agent coding loops.
templates.when_to_use: Use for TypeScript/JavaScript services, Next.js or Node APIs, npm/pnpm monorepos, and any task where the agent needs a modern Node toolchain. Prefer when the repo is primarily JS/TS.
templates.when_not_to_use: Do not use for pure Python, data science notebooks, or ML training. Prefer fullstack-web if the task is specifically Next.js UI + API scaffolding with web CLIs.

templates.name: Python
templates.summary: Python 3.12 with uv, pip, and pytest for agent-driven Python work.
templates.when_to_use: Use for Python libraries, FastAPI/Django apps, scripts, pytest suites, and general backend Python tasks. Good default when the task language is Python and not heavy ML.
templates.when_not_to_use: Do not use for Node/TS frontends. Prefer data-ml when the task needs notebooks, pandas/sklearn-heavy workflows, or training loops.

templates.name: Full-stack web
templates.summary: Node 22 plus web-oriented CLIs for Next.js and full-stack app work.
templates.when_to_use: Use when building or refactoring web apps (Next.js, React, Tailwind, shadcn-style UIs), landing pages, dashboards, or full-stack TypeScript products. Includes common frontend build tools.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer leaner node-ts for pure API/backend Node work. Prefer python/data-ml for non-web stacks.

templates.name: Data / ML
templates.summary: Python ML/data basics: uv, jupyter, pandas, and common scientific packages.
templates.when_to_use: Use for data analysis, notebook exploration, feature engineering, lightweight model training, and evaluation scripts. Prefer when the agent needs pandas/numpy/sklearn or Jupyter.
templates.when_not_to_use: Do not use for GPU-heavy large model training (not provisioned here). Prefer python for plain app/API Python without data stack bloat.

templates.name: Agent shell
templates.summary: Minimal Debian shell with git and curl for any coding agent.
templates.when_to_use: Use as a generic sandbox when the task language is unclear, the agent only needs git/shell, or you want the smallest image to install tools at runtime. Safe default for exploratory tasks.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer a stack-specific image (node-ts, python, fullstack-web, data-ml) once the language/runtime is known — those boot faster for real coding.

templates.name: Claude devbox
templates.summary: Node + Python toolchain tuned for Claude Code / Anthropic agent loops.
templates.when_to_use: Use when the primary agent is Claude (Claude Code, Anthropic API agents) and the task is general software engineering. Includes Node and Python so Claude can work across common stacks without switching images.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer stack-specific images if you already know a single runtime and want a smaller image. Prefer other *-devbox images when a different agent brand is driving the session.

templates.name: Codex devbox
templates.summary: Node + Python environment shaped for Codex-style coding agents.
templates.when_to_use: Use when OpenAI Codex (or Codex-like agents) will drive the session — refactors, tests, and multi-file edits across JS/TS and Python repos.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer claude-devbox / cursor-workspace / gemini-devbox when those agents are primary. Prefer node-ts or python for single-stack cost/size.

templates.name: Cursor workspace
templates.summary: Full-stack friendly image for Cursor agent / remote workspace sessions.
templates.when_to_use: Use when Cursor is the primary agent or human+agent IDE session — web apps, TypeScript monorepos, and interactive debugging alongside the agent.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer agent-shell for tiny tasks. Prefer data-ml for ML-only work. Prefer other agent boxes when Cursor is not involved.

templates.name: Grok bench
templates.summary: Lean Node + Python box for Grok / xAI-oriented agent loops and benchmarks.
templates.when_to_use: Use when Grok (xAI) is the driving agent, especially for benchmarking, eval harnesses, or long-running coding loops that need Node and Python.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer stack-specific images for single-language production app work. Prefer other agent boxes when Grok is not the agent.

templates.name: Gemini devbox
templates.summary: Node + Python toolchain for Gemini CLI / Google agent workflows.
templates.when_to_use: Use when Gemini (Google AI / Gemini CLI) is the primary agent for coding, refactors, or multi-language engineering tasks.
templates.when_not_to_use: Prefer other agent-branded images when Gemini is not involved. Prefer node-ts/python when a smaller single-stack image is enough.

templates.name: Ollama (local LLM)
templates.summary: Local qwen2.5-coder:1.5b served via Ollama for on-box, offline codegen.
templates.when_to_use: Use when the task wants an on-box model — offline code generation, no hosted API key, or privacy-sensitive work. The small qwen2.5-coder:1.5b is pulled at runtime and served over the Ollama API.
templates.when_not_to_use: Do not use for heavy model training or when a hosted model is fine and faster. CPU-only inference is slow (seconds per response); prefer an agent-branded devbox when a cloud LLM drives the session.
